headline: 'In the war between the US and Iran, both sides are convinced they control the clock'
description: Tehran believes Trump needs a deal; Washington thinks the Islamic Republic cannot survive without one, explains Alain Frachon in his column for Le Monde.

headline: Anger grows among London's Jewish community as antisemitic attacks surge
US Supreme Court undermines a civil rights cornerstone: Redistricting to represent minorities
Musk's irritation at questions about OpenAI's origin on display at trial
description: Elon Musk, CEO of Tesla and SpaceX, has accused OpenAI CEO Sam Altman of having diverted a non-profit foundation they created together in 2015. On Wednesday, he faced his own emails and predictions from the time in court.

headline: Sarkozy hits back with new evidence at appeal trial over alleged Libyan funding
description: While the former French president was expected to react on Wednesday to a damning statement by his former associate, Claude Guéant, his lawyers instead submitted figures claiming that half of the sum the Libyans had sent was paid back to them.

headline: Russia drastically scales back May 9 parade over fears of Ukrainian drone attacks
description: For the first time since 2008, the victory parade will feature no military hardware. In recent months, Ukraine has repeatedly carried out drone strikes deep inside Russia.

headline: At the Musée Picasso Paris, Henry Taylor and the emergence of African American modernity
description: A hundred works by the artist reflect the influences that shaped part of his journey and capture, with intensity, the lives of individuals he encountered as well as the history of an entire country.

Bardella's new economic advisor faces questions over his business track record
description: François Durvye has claimed to have doubled the far-right billionaire Pierre-Edouard Stérin's net worth when he managed his 'family office.' However, several elements have raised doubts over the credibility of his figures, as most of his growth projects never came to fruition.

headline: Bologna, a symbol of progressive Italy, is targeted by the far right
description: Heir to humanist ideas and an antifascist culture established after World War II, the capital of Emilia-Romagna has become a laboratory for resistance to reactionary ideas in Italy.

headline: Manon Fleury, Michelin-starred chef: 'I constantly thought about cooking because I was anorexic'
description: A former junior French fencing champion, the chef talks about how her eating disorder led her to gastronomy, and, eventually, a Michelin star.

headline: King Charles III gives President Trump a lesson in democracy
